title: Add Minutes Across an Hour
description: Add a number of minutes greater than 60 to a start time by converting 60 minutes to 1 hour and adjusting the remaining minutes.
condition: A start time and a duration in minutes greater than 60.
response: Add 60 minutes as 1 hour, add remaining minutes, and state the new time.
label: Principle / Model
example: Start 2:45 p.m., add 95 minutes: 95 = 60 + 35, so 2:45 + 1 hour = 3:45, then +35 minutes = 4:20 p.m.

title: Compare Fractions with Same Denominator
description: Compare fractions with the same denominator by comparing numerators.
response: The fraction with the larger numerator is greater.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compare Fractions with Same Numerator
description: Compare fractions with the same numerator by comparing denominators.
response: The fraction with the smaller denominator is greater.
label: Principle / Model
example: \(\frac{3}{6} > \frac{3}{8}\) because sixths are larger than eighths.

title: Distributive Property for Products
description: Decompose a factor into tens and ones to multiply using the distributive property.
condition: A product with a two-digit factor (e.g., a×(b+c)).
response: Multiply each part and add partial products.
label: Principle / Model

title: Division as Equal Groups
description: Interpret division as either finding number in each group or number of groups.
condition: A total and either number of groups or size of each group is given.
response: Write or solve a division equation to find the unknown.
label: Principle / Model
example: \(35 \div 7 = 5\) means 35 students split into 7 equal groups gives 5 in each group.

title: Find Factor Pairs
description: Identify pairs of whole numbers whose product equals a given number within 100.
response: List pairs of factors that multiply to the number.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Non-verbal classification assumes fluency-based retrieval; could be verbal rule ???

title: Generate Equivalent Fractions
description: Create or recognize equivalent fractions by multiplying or dividing numerator and denominator by the same number.
condition: A fraction and a factor relating numerator and denominator.
response: Multiply or divide numerator and denominator by the same number.
label: Principle / Model
example: \(\frac{1}{2} = \frac{3}{6}\) by multiplying top and bottom by 3.

title: Interpret Area Model for Fractions
description: Determine whether a shaded region represents a given fraction by checking equal partitions and shaded parts.
condition: A shape partitioned into equal parts with some shaded.
response: Count shaded parts over total equal parts and compare to target fraction.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 2 of 3 equal parts are shaded, the fraction is \(\frac{2}{3}\).

title: Partition Number Line into Equal Parts
description: Divide the interval from 0 to 1 into equal parts based on the denominator and locate fractions.
condition: A number line with 0 and 1 labeled and a target fraction a/b.
response: Partition into b equal parts and mark the ath tick.
label: Principle / Model
example: To plot \(\frac{1}{4}\), divide 0–1 into 4 equal parts and mark the first tick.

title: Represent Context with Equation
description: Translate a word problem involving equal groups into a multiplication or division equation with an unknown.
condition: A word problem describing equal groups or arrays.
response: Write a corresponding equation using a symbol for the unknown.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract Using Compensation
description: Subtract by adjusting to a friendly number and compensating.
condition: A subtraction within 1,000 where adjusting by a nearby benchmark (e.g., 100) is helpful.
response: Adjust minuend or subtrahend to a friendly number, then compensate.
label: Principle / Model

title: Write Whole Number as Fraction
description: Express a whole number as a fraction with denominator 1 or as an equivalent fraction.
response: Write the number as n/1 or an equivalent fraction.
label: Principle / Model
